title: The 2026 AI Index highlights the gap between capability and preparedness
friendlyTitle: A major AI report says society is still catching up
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Index Report 2026 argues that AI capabilities are advancing faster than governance frameworks, evaluation methods, education systems, and impact-measurement infrastructure. This edition adds more coverage of economic value, labor-market effects, AI sovereignty, science, and medicine.
friendlySummary: The 2026 AI Index says AI is improving quickly, but rules, education, evaluation, and data systems are not keeping up at the same speed.
whyItMatters: For leaders and practitioners, the report is a reminder to track organizational readiness, not just model benchmarks. Daily AI adoption depends on evaluation, training, data governance, and realistic measurement of where AI is helping or creating risk.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because using AI well is not only about better models. People and organizations also need better training, checks, and planning.
